LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HER IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE DEFAULT ENTERED ON SEPTEMBER 25, 2018 IS SET ASIDE AND MS. SULLIVAN-GREEN MUST RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 3, 2018. COUNSEL FOR MS. SULLIVAN-GREEN IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. NEITHER OF THE TWO CLAIMS ARE A "TRUST CONTEST" PER THE PROBATE CODE BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS ARE NEITHER TRUSTEES NOR BENEFICIARIES UNDER THE RESTATEMENT OF THE TRUST. THEREFORE, THE PROBATE DIVISION DOES NOT HAVE EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION OF THIS CASE AND THE LIMITATIONS PERIOD OF PROBATE CODE 16061.8 IS INAPPLICABLE TO THE TWO CLAIMS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFFS. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH EXPECTED INHERITANCE IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED PER BECKWITH V. DAHL (2012) 205 CAL.APP.4TH 1039. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, E-MAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM.(L&M302)

DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PRIOR SEPARATE TRIAL OF SPECIAL DEFENSES IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SERVE AND FILE THE MOTION AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AS REQUIRED BY CODE CIV. PROC., 1005(B). THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT EVEN IF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUE HAD NOT ALREADY BEEN DECIDED BY ITS ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER, THE COURT WOULD DENY ANY MOTION FOR A SEPARATE TRIAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ITS RENEWAL BEFORE THE ASSIGNED TRIAL JUDGE. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Defendant, Terry Sullivan-Greens Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Prior Separate Trial Of Special Defenses, Supporting Declaration; And Supporting Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S Motion For Order Granting Prior Separate Trial Of Special Defenses.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Terry Sullivan-Green's Motion For Order Granting Prior Separate Trial Of Special Defenses is off calendar. Defendant failed to serve and file the motion at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code Civ. Proc., 1005(b). The Court further notes that even if the statute of limitations issue had not already been decided by its order overruling defendant's demurrer, the court would deny any motion for a separate trial without prejudice to its renewal before the assigned trial judge.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2019 Continued To Mar-13-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to both causes of action. Neither of the two claims are a "trust contest" per the Probate Code because plaintiffs are neither trustees nor beneficiaries under the restatement of the trust. Therefore, the probate division does not have exclusive jurisdiction of this case and the limitations period of Probate Code 16061.8 is inapplicable to the two claims alleged by plaintiffs. The second cause of action for intentional interference with expected inheritance is adequately alleged per Beckwith v. Dahl (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 1039. Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.com.(L&M302)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TERRY SULLIVAN-GREEN'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Defendant Terry Sullivan-Green's motion to set aside the default entered against her is granted. No opposition filed. The default entered on September 25, 2018 is set aside and Ms. Sullivan-Green must respond to the complaint no later than December 3, 2018.

Counsel for Ms. Sullivan-Green is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
